NB: what exactly is correct URL or URI ?!
In any case I think these should be the same for these two variables:

Google said: "The main difference between a URL and a URI is that a URL specifies the location of a resource on the internet, while a URI can be used to identify any type of resource, not just those on the internet. URI is usually used in XML, tag library files, and other files, such as JSTL and XSTL."

Is there a reason to subclass a Spring framework class instead of using composition?

This should not be a path variable!
The metrics produced look like this:

    /{dmiBasePath}/v1/ch/{cmHandleId}/{resourceName}

where in the Jira ticket they want metrics like this:

    /dmi/v1/ch/{cmHandleId}/{resourceName}

so basically line above should be:

    pathSegment(dmiBasePath)

This test and the one below make no sense anymore, because the new DmiServiceUrlBuilder doesn't URL encode anything! (The WebClient does it.)

For example, the segment some/special?characters=are\\\\encoded
has an expectedUrlTemplate of:

    myDmiServer/{dmiBasePath}/v1/some/special?characters=are\\encoded/{myVariableSegment}

which is clearly NOT encoded.

I'm not sure what to do here, maybe just remove the test?

Hmm, now that I think about it, the fixed path segments SHOULD be encoded! i.e. the original test was correct, and this is wrong.
Maybe we should have discussion about this.
agreed, so that also means the dmiPasePath needs to be removed from the variabel(parameter)  map, correct?

Yeah I think we need to have a closer look.
but I now think we dont have any 'fixed' parts with special characters (that would be  bad design). And as the URL build doesn't encode anymore. this tets can probably me removed.
Can we have a test that the WEbCLient does what we expect (technically we dont need to as it is a 3pp but it might be good to show it...)
Agreed, fixed parts should not have special characters.
Nope, It should be used as a composition only.
NB: this might be better doen in a separate test just like the test starting on line #57
NB: I would NOT use  a grooy string here but hard-code the expected value for dmiBasePath here
NB: legacy use @Shared def (Groovy, not Java !)
like comment in previous test just hardcode the excepted values for easier to read and understand test
At  #57 def 'Build URL template parameters with empty query parameters.'() does cover this scenario and can be removed from here.
